sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.60 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: We say that$G$is a virtually compact special group if$G$has a finite-index subgroup which is isomorphic to the fundamental group of a compact special complex (in the sense of F.\,Haglund, D.\,T.\,Wise, Geom. Funct. Anal., 17, no.\,5 (2008), 1551--1620). Let$G$be a virtual retract of a finitely generated right-angled Artin group. Must$G$be a virtually compact special group? An affirmative answer wo...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
